topic: Schumann suppression G3 storm — post-V51.1 detail_evidence rewrite
change_summary: detail_evidence grew 1325 -> 2951 chars (+122%) since c2 on 2026-04-08. Added V51.1 Update paragraph describing dome's acknowledgement of W027 falsification and replacement with PRED-SR-SUPPRESS. Added Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 citation alongside Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 (c2 recommendation partially applied). Added mention of dashboard 'Unscored' status.
our_argument_summary: Our review still argues Self-Contradicted on three grounds: (1) dome's cavity formula predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not the observed 7.83 Hz; (2) no dome formula predicts the specific storm-time frequency shift or amplitude change; (3) PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is a post-hoc retrodiction after the original W027 (elevation) prediction failed. The new V51.1 Update paragraph adds two secondary claims: that storm-time SR suppression 'is exactly what the established ionospheric mechanism predicts' (citing Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002), and that PRED-SR-SUPPRESS merely borrows standard physics. Verdict: Self-Contradicted remains correct on the baseline-frequency grounds, but the new V51.1 paragraph contains factual errors that weaken the overall argument.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt — Domain 9 'Schumann Amplitude Suppression' (still 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed' as of the raw-text snapshot)
data/predictions.json — PRED-SR-SUPPRESS entry (registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days after March 20-22 G3 storm)
monitor/changes/chg-20260408-1014-004.json — poller detection of dome's W027 falsification narrative
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 9 falsification criterion: 'G3+ storm (Kp>=7) does NOT cause >30% amplitude drop within 6 hours.' No trigger condition ever met per dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 269). The March 20-22 G3 storm went unscored despite meeting the Kp>=7 threshold per NOAA SWPC — either the Kp-detection logic missed it, the author opted not to score, or the dashboard text is cached. No dome formula derives a suppression magnitude from H(r), H_0, or lambda_g.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither. The stated claim is a conditional whose trigger has never fired in the dome's own view. Structurally identical to c2 finding: test never ran, dome counts it as confirmed regardless.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Same as c2 — double escape: rare-event trigger plus unscored-status. New wrinkle: an actual G3 storm DID occur March 20-22 but the monitor says 'no G3+ storm observed' — this is a cherry-pick escape by selective non-detection.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our current text now correctly notes the 'Unscored' status (c2 recommendation applied). However, the text frames this as passive ('the dome's own Live Power Dashboard scores this WIN as Unscored') without flagging the stronger point from the PRED-SR-SUPPRESS assessment: the March G3 storm occurred but went unscored by the monitor, which either misses the threshold or selectively skips events.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 9 in the dome's live power dashboard remains 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 269). The falsification criterion never fires. Monitoring infrastructure exists as a stub.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Storm-time Schumann perturbations are a well-studied ionospheric phenomenon. The dome renames the cause ('aetheric perturbation') without supplying a new numerical prediction. Note: the DIRECTION of the standard prediction (enhancement vs suppression) is actually contested in the literature — Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 and Pazos et al. 2019 report amplitude INCREASES during SPEs and geomagnetic storms. Our current text oversimplifies this to 'suppression is standard physics.' See HOLE 1 below. The 'relabels_standard' tag is still true because the phenomenon class (storm-SR coupling) is in the literature; the specific direction is where our review gets it wrong.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: SR storm responses studied since the 1960s-70s (Sentman 1990; Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999; Nickolaenko et al. 2004; Pazos et al. 2019). Stronger: PRED-SR-SUPPRESS itself was registered 2026-03-23, just 1-3 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 storm — the dome had real-time SR data access during the storm and registered the replacement prediction after the event it claims to predict. This upgrades 'post-hoc' from 'adopts known observation' to 'registers a prediction after the specific event.'
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome equation derives a suppression magnitude, timing, or frequency shift from H(r), H_0, lambda_g, or any aetheric parameter. Same as c2.

holes_found.description: Our text misrepresents Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999. The cited paper ('Schumann resonance parameter changes during high-energy particle precipitation,' JGR 104, 10111-10118, DOI 10.1029/1999JA900056) actually found INCREASE of resonance frequency and DECREASE of damping during solar proton events at Arrival Heights, Antarctica — i.e., amplitude ENHANCEMENT, not suppression. Our V51.1 Update paragraph uses this citation to support the claim that 'suppression during G3+ storms is exactly what the established ionospheric mechanism predicts.' Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 does NOT support that claim — it supports the OPPOSITE direction. Pazos et al. 2019 (DOI 10.1016/j.jastp.2019.105091) also reports statistically significant amplitude INCREASES at Coeneo, Mexico during storms. A dome defender reading our text, clicking the citation, and finding enhancement findings can argue: (a) our review misrepresents its own sources, (b) the dome's ORIGINAL W027 (elevation) was empirically defensible, (c) the V51.1 replacement to SUPPRESSION is actually farther from the literature consensus than the original. This critical hole is the single most exploitable weakness in the WIN-061 argument as currently written.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the V51.1 Update paragraph to acknowledge the literature is mixed. Replace 'suppression during G3+ storms is exactly what the established ionospheric mechanism predicts' with language like: 'The storm-SR relationship is studied but the direction is not uniform across events — Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 found frequency increase and damping decrease during SPEs; Pazos et al. 2019 reported amplitude increases during moderate-to-severe storms; other studies report suppression depending on storm phase, location, and Schumann mode.' Then make the stronger point: the dome provides no quantitative formula that would predict which direction or which magnitude is expected — it just asserts whatever was observed last. This is non-falsifiable retroactively-tunable framing, which is a more durable criticism than 'this is standard physics.'

holes_found.description: The DOI in the citation link is wrong. The text has href='https://doi.org/10.1029/1999JA900037' for Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999, but the correct DOI is 10.1029/1999JA900056. The incorrect DOI appears twice in the paragraph (both the first 'storm-suppression physics' citation and the 'This is documented in Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999' citation). A dome defender clicking the link may land on a different paper (or a broken page), instantly undermining our citation credibility. This is the exact kind of fabricated/broken citation we have criticized the dome model for (Part 4 code analysis).
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '10.1029/1999JA900037' with '10.1029/1999JA900056' in both places. Verify the DOI resolves to 'Schumann resonance parameter changes during high-energy particle precipitation,' J. Geophys. Res. 104 (A5), 10111-10118, 1999.

holes_found.description: The mechanism description in the V51.1 paragraph is physically backwards of what the cited paper measured. Our text says: 'storm-injected particles increase D-region conductivity, lowering the cavity Q-factor and reducing amplitude.' Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 measured DECREASED damping (which is INCREASED Q-factor, Q = 1/damping) and INCREASED frequency. Decreased damping means less energy loss, which corresponds to HIGHER amplitude, not lower. Our mechanism text and our cited evidence are inconsistent. Either the mechanism is wrong or the citation is wrong — both cannot be true simultaneously. An adversarial physics reader can spot this in seconds.
holes_found.recommendation: If we want to defend 'suppression is standard physics' we need to cite a paper that actually shows suppression and describe the mechanism that paper invokes. If we want to cite Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999, we need to describe their mechanism accurately (enhanced Q via reduced damping from ionization of the cavity upper boundary) and change the argumentative framing away from 'suppression is standard.' The cleanest fix is the reframing in HOLE 1: the literature is mixed, so the dome's assertion-of-observation is not derivable from 'standard physics' either way.

holes_found.description: Our V51.1 Update paragraph accepts the dome's narrative claim that 'when a G3+ storm was observed, the measured Schumann response was ~35% suppression' without question. But the dome's own Live Power Dashboard (Domain 9) reports 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed' — as our same paragraph notes. These are incompatible statements about the dome's own data: either a G3 storm was observed and scored (yielding the ~35% suppression we mention), or no G3 storm was observed (yielding Unscored). The PRED-SR-SUPPRESS analyst assessment (monitor/analyst/expansions/PRED-assessment-PRED-SR-SUPPRESS.json) notes the March 20-22 G3 storm occurred per NOAA SWPC but the dome's monitor missed or skipped it. We should surface this directly rather than implicitly accepting two contradictory dome claims.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence to the V51.1 Update paragraph along the lines of: 'The dome simultaneously claims (a) a G3 storm produced ~35% SR suppression justifying the W027 falsification narrative, and (b) its automated monitor shows Unscored with no G3+ storm observed. These cannot both be true. The March 20-22 G3 storm (NOAA SWPC, Kp=7) either triggered the monitor or did not — the dome presents both framings simultaneously.' This strengthens the Self-Contradicted verdict with an internal-consistency attack and removes our implicit endorsement of the dome's ~35% claim.

holes_found.description: The timestamp-forward nature of PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is omitted from our text. Per data/predictions.json, PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered 2026-03-23 — 1-3 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 storm. The dome had real-time access to SR amplitude data during the storm (HeartMath GCI API, near-real-time). Registering a prediction 1-3 days after observing the event it claims to predict is a much sharper criticism than 'post-hoc retrodiction of standard physics.' The PRED-SR-SUPPRESS analyst assessment captures this; WIN-061 should reference it.
holes_found.recommendation: Add language like: 'PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days AFTER the March 20-22 G3 geomagnetic storm (NOAA SWPC confirmed, Kp=7). Real-time SR data was available during the storm. A prediction registered after the specific event it claims to predict is not a prospective prediction; it is an observation with a future date label.' This is a durable, direct criticism with a verifiable timestamp trail.

holes_found.description: The V51.1 Update is dated '2026-04-08' but that is the poller DETECTION date (chg-20260408-1014-004.json), not the dome's actual update date. The dome's prediction swap is traceable to no later than 2026-03-23 (when PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered). Labeling the update '2026-04-08' is minor but may feed a defender narrative that our review misrepresents timelines.
holes_found.recommendation: Change '(2026-04-08)' to something like '(detected 2026-04-08; PRED-SR-SUPPRESS registered 2026-03-23)' or simply '(V51.1)' without a specific date.

holes_found.description: The H_eff averaging method is still unspecified (carry-over from c1 and c2 findings). Our '~22 Hz' uses simple linear average H_eff = 3,316 km (ratio 2.89x over 7.83 Hz). Area-weighted gives 35.1 Hz (4.49x) and pole-only 8.8 Hz (1.12x). Without disclosure of the averaging choice, a sophisticated defender can argue we cherry-picked the method that maximized apparent overshoot. The simple-average is defensible, but that defense has to be in the text.
holes_found.recommendation: One-sentence footnote: 'H_eff = 3,316 km is the simple linear average of H(r) over the disc from 0 to R = 20,015 km; area-weighted averaging gives 35 Hz (worse for the dome), pole-only gives 8.8 Hz (closer but physically unjustifiable as a cavity mean).' This disclosure both honestly notes the method and shows the dome's number isn't improved by any reasonable averaging choice.

holes_found.description: The detail_verdict_text field is still only 88 characters ('Schumann suppression is ionospheric physics; dome predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not 7.83 Hz.') — this is the detail view's one-line verdict. Given the new V51.1 context, it should briefly note the post-registration prediction timing and the baseline overshoot. The current text only covers the baseline argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand detail_verdict_text to roughly: 'Dome geometry predicts ~22 Hz baseline, not the observed 7.83 Hz; the replacement prediction PRED-SR-SUPPRESS was registered 1-3 days after the G3 storm it claims to predict; no dome formula derives a suppression magnitude.' Keep it concise — detail_verdict_text should be a ~200-char summary, not a restatement of detail_evidence.

stronger_arguments.description: Make the timing attack the primary V51.1 criticism. 'Post-hoc' isn't wrong but it's weak against a motivated defender. 'Registered 1-3 days after the specific event it claims to predict' is a much sharper claim with a verifiable timestamp. This should lead the V51.1 paragraph, before the standard-physics framing (which, per HOLE 1, is factually shaky anyway).
sources: NOAA SWPC Kp archive for March 2026

stronger_arguments.description: The WIN-038 tension from c2 remains unused. WIN-038 claims Schumann stability at 7.83 +/- 0.3 Hz. Storm-time shifts documented by Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 are ~0.3-1.0 Hz — explicitly exceeding the WIN-038 stability window. WIN-038 and WIN-061 cannot both be 'confirmed' because they describe mutually incompatible behavior during the same physical events.

The copper firmament / WIN-034 contradiction (from c1 and c2) remains unused. If WIN-034 claims the firmament is copper/bronze, and copper conductivity ~6e7 S/m is constant under particle precipitation, then a copper dome should not show storm-time boundary-conductivity changes. This is an internal geometric-material contradiction independent of the frequency baseline.
sources: material properties: copper electrical conductivity constant across geomagnetic storm conditions

kernel_of_truth.description: Storm-time Schumann resonance changes are real and well-studied. The dome model correctly identifies that the SR spectrum responds to geomagnetic disturbances. In particular, SR amplitude enhancement (increased first-mode amplitude) during moderate-to-severe storms is documented at multiple stations (Pazos et al. 2019 at Coeneo, Mexico; Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 for damping-decrease during SPEs).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome's current claim is 30%+ amplitude SUPPRESSION — which is opposite of the most-cited direction in the literature. The dome provides no equation that would select suppression over enhancement, no magnitude prediction from geometry, no baseline frequency agreement (dome geometry predicts 8.8-35 Hz range, not 7.83 Hz), no monitor that actually scored the claimed event (March 2026 G3 went Unscored), and a timestamp showing the prediction was registered 1-3 days after the event it 'predicts.' The phenomenon being real does not validate a specific directional prediction registered after the fact.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who has read our six methodological principles would construct this defense: 'Your Self-Contradicted verdict rests on two pillars: (a) the ~22 Hz baseline overshoot from H_eff = 3,316 km, and (b) the claim that PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is already covered by standard physics. Pillar (b) fails your own discriminatory-power test. Your cited paper (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999) actually documents amplitude enhancement during SPEs via reduced damping — the opposite of what you claim it supports. Pazos et al. 2019 likewise finds increases. You simultaneously cite these papers as authority for suppression being standard physics and criticize the dome for post-hoc retrodiction of standard physics — but the standard physics direction you cite is the opposite of the dome claim. So PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is either (i) wrong, in which case it's not retrodicting standard physics, or (ii) right but contrary to the papers you cite. Either way your post-hoc argument is inconsistent with your own evidence. This doesn't rescue the dome — the ~22 Hz baseline overshoot (pillar a) still stands — but it means the Self-Contradicted verdict is built partly on a strawman of the dome's V51.1 update, not on the dome's actual position.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix HOLES 1-3 simultaneously. Reframe the V51.1 paragraph around two durable criticisms: (i) the timestamp-forward registration (1-3 days after the event), and (ii) the dome's missing mechanism / mismatched monitor. Drop or heavily qualify the 'standard physics predicts suppression' claim — the literature does not actually support it uniformly, and citing Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 to defend it is backwards. The ~22 Hz baseline argument is solid and can carry the Self-Contradicted verdict on its own.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No direct contradictions across WIN-061's three Schumann neighbors (002, 029, 038) — they all carry Self-Contradicted and invoke the ~22 Hz dome-cavity prediction. One subtle consistency risk: WIN-002 discusses 10.59 Hz (Schumann 1952 globe-theoretical value the dome reframes as 'pre-damping') while WIN-061 discusses ~22 Hz (dome cavity formula prediction). These are genuinely different numbers for different arguments, but a confused reader could conflate them. A defender could say 'your review uses multiple dome baselines — pick one.' This is not an inconsistency in our argument, but a cross-reference to WIN-002 explaining the distinction would help. Also: the WIN-034 copper-firmament and WIN-038 stability tensions remain unused, meaning WIN-061 is not yet leveraging the full cross-WIN case our own other arguments could support.

claims_checked: 'dome gets the base frequency wrong by 2.8x' ratio claim
'~30% amplitude reduction during G3 events' (attributed to dome narrative)
quantitative_verification.computations: Dome formula H(r) = 8537*exp(-r/8619), R = 20015. Simple-linear-average H over [0, R] gives H_eff = 3,316 km (not 3,400 — rounded in our text). f = c/(4*H) = 300000/(4*3316) = 22.62 Hz, rounds to ~22 Hz. Ratio 22.6/7.83 = 2.89x (our text says '2.8x' which rounds correctly). Area-weighted H gives 2,135 km -> f = 35.1 Hz. Pole-only H = 8,537 km -> f = 8.79 Hz (still above 7.83 Hz by 12%). All three averaging choices give baselines ABOVE the observed 7.83 Hz. Our quantitative claim survives any reasonable averaging method. DOI check: 10.1029/1999JA900037 does NOT match Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 'Schumann resonance parameter changes during high-energy particle precipitation' (correct DOI is 10.1029/1999JA900056, JGR 104 A5, 10111-10118). Our text's DOI is wrong — citation broken.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two discrepancies: (1) DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 in our text is wrong; correct DOI is 10.1029/1999JA900056 (HOLE 2). (2) H_eff = 3,400 km in our text is a rounded restatement of the true simple-average 3,316 km; the calculation is internally consistent but the rounded figure is used without method disclosure (HOLE 7 severity moderate).

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 re-review of WIN-061 triggered by change-detection (detail_evidence 1325 -> 2951, +122%). The new content is a V51.1 Update paragraph responding to the dome's acknowledgement of the W027 falsification and its replacement with PRED-SR-SUPPRESS.

STATUS OF C2 RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 citation: ADDED (c2 recommended this) — but with the WRONG DOI (10.1029/1999JA900037 instead of 10.1029/1999JA900056). Net effect is worse than having no citation at all, because a dome defender clicking the link will find our citation broken.
- 'Unscored' dashboard argument: ADDED (c2 recommended this) — but framed passively rather than exploited as the internal-consistency contradiction it is.
- WIN-038 stability tension: NOT ADDED.
- WIN-034 copper firmament contradiction: NOT ADDED.
- H_eff averaging method: NOT DISCLOSED.

NEW CONTENT ANALYSIS:
The V51.1 Update paragraph attempts to neutralize the dome's 'we honestly acknowledged falsification' framing by saying the replacement prediction is just standard physics. This is a reasonable rhetorical move, but the specific argument is executed poorly:

1. The 'suppression is standard physics' claim is factually wrong-direction. Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 (our cited evidence) finds Q-factor increase (amplitude enhancement) during SPEs. Pazos et al. 2019 finds amplitude increase at Coeneo. The literature direction during storms is not uniformly suppression. Our text's certainty is misplaced.

2. The mechanism description (higher conductivity -> lower Q -> reduced amplitude) is also the opposite direction of what Schlegel & Füllekrug measured (lower damping -> higher Q -> enhanced amplitude). The internal physics of our paragraph is inconsistent with its own citation.

3. The strongest available argument against PRED-SR-SUPPRESS is not 'standard physics' but timing: the prediction was registered 2026-03-23, 1-3 days after the March 20-22 G3 storm (NOAA SWPC Kp=7). The dome had real-time access to SR amplitude data during the storm. Our current text mentions post-hoc in general terms but omits the specific timestamp arithmetic that makes this a sharp criticism.

4. The Unscored-status argument is included but softly framed. The harder point is that the dome simultaneously claims (a) a G3+ storm observation yielded ~35% suppression, and (b) the monitor shows 'no G3+ storm observed' — these two framings cannot both be true. This is a clean internal-consistency attack the text doesn't make.

RECOMMENDED ACTION: major_rewrite of the V51.1 Update paragraph. The Self-Contradicted verdict is correct on baseline-overshoot grounds alone, but the current V51.1 paragraph actively weakens the argument by building on a misrepresented citation. Risk of keeping the text as-is: a dome defender can credibly argue our review misrepresents its own sources, turning WIN-061 into a talking point against our credibility.

PRIORITY ORDER FOR REWRITE:
(a) Fix DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 -> 10.1029/1999JA900056 (trivial edit, critical for citation integrity)
(b) Reframe V51.1 paragraph away from 'standard physics predicts suppression' toward 'prediction registered after the event + no dome mechanism + monitor blind to the triggering storm'
(c) Add the 'Unscored-but-also-observed' internal contradiction
(d) Optional but helpful: bring in the WIN-038 stability and WIN-034 copper-firmament tensions from the c1/c2 unused-argument list

Verdict confidence drops slightly from c2 (0.94 -> 0.82) because the new V51.1 paragraph introduces exploitable holes. The baseline-overshoot core is unchanged and rock-solid; the peripheral new content is where the weaknesses live.

citations_verified: Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 chapter cited via DOI 10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 — Valid. Resolves to 'Resonances in the Earth-Ionosphere Cavity,' Springer Atmospheric Sciences. General cavity-physics chapter — authoritative but not specific to storm-time suppression.
citations_failed: DOI 10.1029/1999JA900037 attributed to 'Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999, JGR 104, 10111' in two places (first and second paragraphs of detail_evidence). This DOI is WRONG. Correct DOI is 10.1029/1999JA900056. The paper's title is 'Schumann resonance parameter changes during high-energy particle precipitation' (Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999, JGR 104 A5, 10111-10118). Critical citation break — link resolves elsewhere or to a broken page.

Even with the correct DOI, the way our text uses Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 misrepresents the paper's finding. The paper reports damping DECREASE and frequency INCREASE during SPEs (enhancement, not suppression). Our text invokes it as support for storm-time suppression being standard physics — the paper supports the opposite direction.
citations_unchecked: Section 2.1 cross-reference (href='#part2' onclick='showTab(selftest)') — structural check only. Accuracy depends on current SEC-2.1 content.
